solar_array.title: Build a Create: New Age Solar Panel array — daytime passive FE generation for supplemental power

battery_buffer.title: Set up a Battery Box buffer between solar array and consumers — store daytime FE for nighttime use

subtitle.text: The reactor runs forever. The sun runs for free. Both are worth having. The battery connects them.

description: CH.10 · THE SOLAR ARRAY
▸ The Nuclear Reactor generates heat round the clock.
Solar Panels generate FE only in daylight.
They are not competing systems.
They are complementary:
Solar covers the surplus. Reactor covers the baseline.
◈ SOLAR PANEL SETUP
└ Place on surface blocks with sky access
└ No shading: adjacent blocks at same height
will reduce output (place panels elevated)
└ Array scaling: FE output multiplies linearly
with panel count
└ Peak output: noon (60 min game day midpoint)
└ Zero output: night, rain, underground
◈ BATTERY BOX BUFFERING
└ CNA Battery Box: FE storage unit
└ Chain multiple boxes in series for larger reserves
└ Solar → Battery Box → Consumer (via wire coil)
└ Battery maintains consumer power during night
and overcast periods without reactor intervention
└ Prioritize: charge battery fully before routing
solar output directly to machines
◈ HYBRID CONFIGURATION
└ Recommended: Solar → Battery Bank → Laser Cutter
(Laser Cutter benefits from battery smoothing)
└ Reactor → Generator Coil → Main Grid
(Reactor handles constant-demand machines)
└ Grid balance: FE surplus from solar charges
battery without overloading reactor output
▸ The Engineers built the first solar array
because they were afraid the reactor would fail.
The reactor never failed.
The solar array outlasted the civilization anyway.
Redundancy is how things survive.
▶ Build a Solar Panel array [check]
▶ Set up a Battery Box buffer [check]
◇ Solar arrays are the best power setup for
outpost machines (distant farms, mining camps).
No shaft infrastructure needed.
Wire coil from base or local panels — either works.
